speaker: MATT
raw_text: He has about five or six years before he becomes the voiceover industry. The living embodiment of it. Absorbs us like end of Akira. So that's going to be fun. Look forward to that next week. Let's see, what else?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Bauza is funny as fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He has never played Dungeons and Dragons before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm legitimately sad that we're gone next week. Just so we can't be in the wings, to watch it in person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll have a lot of road time to watch it on the way back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With Bauza at the table, any celebrity could show up. He can impersonate anybody. Anybody.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: High Hierophant Ophera of the Birthheart. There was Earthbreaker Groon. There were Maidens of the Raven. Eventually, Allura and Kima, and J'mon Sa Ord had also come into the fray. During this discussion about what to do, what to handle, you were granted a ring by High Bearer Vord that, within a certain radius, could prevent divinations or specifically scrying on you. Not the School of Divination, but magics that divine your location, whatever's in your vicinity. This moment, the council was interrupted by an image of Vecna taunting you, until it was dispelled by Allura. The discussion was that something was going on in the mountain range where Vecna seemed to have Thar Amphala, which had been discovered by a couple of surviving wyvern riders. Thinking about the two possible locations that could be of interest to Vecna, you gathered your materials, your preparations, and with the first striking lot of wyvern riders, on the back of the newly transformed J'mon Sa Ord into the Devo'ssa dragon form, you were transported via druids to the northern icy landscape of Issylra, to the base of the Zenwick Mountains. You discovered that the Crypts of Thomara, an ancient, forgotten dwarven society that had destroyed itself through cannibalism and madness was now a sinkhole in the mountain range, and a series of large, heavy, 150-plus-foot-across cavernous openings in the ground formed a pattern that you soon discovered were footfalls. Steps. You came upon the risen undead primordial earth titan, long left beneath the surface of the world, dead since the Founding, now given unlife and walking its way southeast towards Vasselheim. Atop its shoulders, you saw Thar Amphala, protected by some sort of magical barrier. There you struggled with a few fleets of flying gloomstalkers that were charging their way towards you. You turned yourself invisible through both Scanlan and J'mon Sa Ord-- J'mon or Devo'ssa: both forms, same person-- you managed to, thanks to a very high roll from Keyleth, discover there was a waterfall or trickle of liquid near the hip of this giant titan. Its giant, four, swinging arms of imminent danger in its vicinity only managed to clip Keyleth and Vax once. Surviving the impact, you all gathered at this ingress right around the hip of the titan, where you leapt off the dragon and proceeded to make your way in. Keyleth, using Control Water, was keeping the heavy flow of an underground river from blowing you off the broken steps that lead into your entry. And that is where we left off. At this time, Keyleth, as you're all standing, looking over your shoulder, you can see the opening where you entered. It's this giant view through this window where you can see down below the mountains and the valleys and the landscapes outside. You can hear the wind blowing though and battering like a heavy, deep whistle. Oh! And we have the rest of our party. Perfect timing. Welcome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, everyone was there.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Early Age of Arcanum, so best estimate from what you knew, you guys both learning of the history of this, well over 1,500 years, if not longer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Investigation, or nature check?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This would not be a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My better one? That doesn't count. No, nope. Four total.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Much of this tunnel seems to be natural cavern, but there were steps when you first came in. There were stone steps that were carved or placed within the dirt that eventually were consumed or covered by silt and loose earth and whatever the river knocked loose and began to flow over it. So you can see both. A lot of this is natural, but there seem to be, at least in the 30 or 40 feet you've made into the tunnel, you've seen elements of civilization, of crafted entry, that merge with the terrain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did we get a time frame of how long ago this crypt was built before? How old this crypt has been around?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys slowly make your way along the path, behind Vax, you look out and occasionally see bits of loose earth that looks looser than most. As you brush it aside, you can see the steps continue. A lot of it's been buried, but this was a staircase that slowly ascended and curved. The movement of the titan seems to have knocked a lot of the loose tunnel to cover it. As you brush it aside, you carefully look about, and you can see that there are occasional symbols of a writing you don't understand, but nothing stands out to you as a trap or a trigger.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I touch the ground, now that we're in the titan? I'm thinking maybe this might be where the Knuckles are from. Can I touch it and see if the oven mitts do anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Their general, faint vibration never stops. There's a perpetual, very minute hum to them. Touching it doesn't seem to have any effect or change. The proximity seems to be triggering some sort of harmonization.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach up and touch my Adam's apple and (vibrating) this place is crazy. Sorry. No, you wouldn't do it? I thought you would.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so make an investigation check, Vax, as you slowly lead the group up this slightly curving tunnel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 27, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I didn't get to sort them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, you should do that now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Chaos. Introducing chaos.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you about two to three minutes to sound this out, which knocks about two to three minutes off of your concentration time for your Control Water spell. But this looks to be carved in as a warning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look familiar to any of us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What languages do you guys know?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why are you even looking over here? I know stick figures. I know charades.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do any of you know Dwarven?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You can read Dwarven now! You can read now, Grog. You can read four- to five-letter words.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I can read Dwarven because of the belt.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But only tiny bits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I might fuck up the bigger pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog, as Vax points out some of these symbols, you sit there and concentrate really hard on one of the carvings in the recently uncovered stone step. As you focus, you begin to sound out the sound to yourself repeatedly. (hesitant syllables) You're trying to find the right way to pronounce it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Baa-ram-ewe. Babe, anyone?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The rough translation from what you can-- spending about five minutes or so breaking out this glyph and a few other glyphs, it says, "Leave. Death here. No further."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Baa-ram-ewe. Babe, anyone?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you about two to three minutes to sound this out, which knocks about two to three minutes off of your concentration time for your Control Water spell. But this looks to be carved in as a warning.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Du. Du hast. Du hast mich. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Strangely, if you could have seen Thomara when it was a highly functioning society, they would have been blasting Rammstein everywhere, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Speak Rammstein and enter.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, these weren't the happy-go-lucky dwarves of old. This was a brutal clan.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It looks like a warning of some kind.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Between all of you guys able to help, it's a negligible period of time, and it wasn't too heavy of an impact. You manage to get them free over the next five or so minutes. They get their breath back. A bit battered, but you get the sense that you should travel quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Dex, huh?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike needs to do it too, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20. Gil coming in in the clutch!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 13 for the other gnome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Everyone but Grog, you glance up just in time to watch as a large piece of the tunnel ceiling dislodges and comes plummeting down in your direction. Grog, you dive out of the way, being clipped off the shoulder, but you manage to avoid most of the impact. The rest of you, however, immediately get buried by rock and broken earth, suffering 29 points of bludgeoning damage to Percival, Pike, and Scanlan. And they are buried and restrained right now, unable to breathe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I go over and I start-- boulders, dirt, everything.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Picking up the pace, you continue up the curvature of the upward ascension. It's probably been about 60 or 70 feet since you've seen any carved portion of terrain, any sign of previous dwarven construct or building or architecture. As the tunnel stops at the curve, it eventually, looking ahead, ascends a bit, about a ten-foot climb up onto a plateau of some kind. You can see some weird root-like tree is growing out of one wall and seems to be dead and petrified in its current space. Do you guys wish to ascend to the plateau?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I go over and I start-- boulders, dirt, everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Between all of you guys able to help, it's a negligible period of time, and it wasn't too heavy of an impact. You manage to get them free over the next five or so minutes. They get their breath back. A bit battered, but you get the sense that you should travel quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll keep going quickly, then.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Pushing close to an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 16 plus five lightning damage. And then, since he is next to Scanlan, I get sneak attack?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, 12. 15 sneak attack! And then Hunter's Mark, three.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Fantastic. 39 damage in one hit. So the second one, as it hits, it causes this rift in the center of its large, battering ram-like arm to shift and crack and pieces of broken shale peel and fall off to the ground around it. Does that end your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to move, or do you want to stay where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to stay where I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Vax, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've been in here under an hour. Probably not much time left, but under an hour, right? Since we arrived?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As it slams with both fists into you, with one giant (whoosh) over the head as it impacts you, you grab with one hand the back of its fist and grin, and then bring the axe straight up into its torso with one hand. It strikes and splits open part of its rock chest and you hear this (guttural wheeze) which it reforms again. But still, it's taken the brunt of the impact, and it's now going to shift into the wall, which you can't attack because you already used your reaction. That ends their turn. Keyleth, you're up. You're currently buried. You're blinded.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, not this guy, they're still walking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And the guy who's right there is going to take two strikes at you, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, bring it. They both hit. They're reckless.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. Yep. And the other attack-- Yep, both hit. So that's 16 points of bludgeoning damage, reduced to eight. And 12 points of bludgeoning damage, reduced to six.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Six. Can I take my retaliation strike against him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Lovely. Reckless, Great Weapon Master. Come on. Natural 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's going to suck!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Squish him! Make a building out of him! I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh! I reroll ones and twos! Great. 11, that's better.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You get all sorts of bonuses to that crit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Now I've got David Bowie in my head.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 48 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, necrotic. 52.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is that doubled? And you doubled everything into your extra crit--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've been practicing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, the wings vanish. You can take a short rest if you guys would like to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It could just be a fight to take away our stuff, before we go to a bigger fight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All you find is the door on the left appears to be unlocked and you could possibly push it open, and you see the dwarven writing on the outside of the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, come on. We're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are we fucking pretending like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we see, well, can Pike, "Can I see into the hovel?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to push the door open and have a look inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's closed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is closed, you can see--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, hold on, hold on. There are runes, of which I can read.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, five minutes go by, and--?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't knock it, all right, just be grateful. Can I try to take a look at the Dwarvish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you roughly 15 or so minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, come on! We cannot wait for 15 minutes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax's wings go (poof).

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right so you guys can take your hit dice as a short rest. Your form reverts, which if you wanted to try and peek within, you couldn't anyway, because it's worked stone so you can't glide through that. The words you pull out from this are, "The last king, stalwart warrior of our mountain secrets, his might unmatched, his will unbroken, his passing invites the madness."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, come on. We're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are we fucking pretending like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we see, well, can Pike, "Can I see into the hovel?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to push the door open and have a look inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's closed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is closed, you can see--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, hold on, hold on. There are runes, of which I can read.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, five minutes go by, and--?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't knock it, all right, just be grateful. Can I try to take a look at the Dwarvish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you roughly 15 or so minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, come on! We cannot wait for 15 minutes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax's wings go (poof).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the wings vanish. You can take a short rest if you guys would like to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: While he's doing that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm Professor X.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It'll take a while, so it means you lose your invisibility--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, might as well.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Grog, you do not get a short rest unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shit, okay, all right. It's been worth the wait. It is the last king, there's like a secret buried with him and his death brought on the madness. So like, there's definitely treasure with him down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Unless it's already been raided?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think he's still in there and he's maybe been turning people mad. That would be my guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The last king.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's a king in there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What, say it again?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He's the last king, there's like, the secret with--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Stalwart warrior of our mountain secrets."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Mountain dwarf?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Stalwart warrior of our mountain secrets.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: "When I'm turning undead, if they're like little piddly guys, they blow up, but if he's a king he might not blow up."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, then we can--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: "So he might get scared of me, or nothing, I don't know?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Double Pike is the best Pike.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's like there's magic in the world, Liam, that allows you to fly 400 feet a round!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you pull the lid up, (trap triggers) the same toxic fume emanates from it but you are not there in the space, instead appearing right next to the central cylindrical platform in the center of the chamber and look back and the cloud slowly dissipates around. The lid has now fallen back into place.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Aw, shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was hot.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I walk over to it (finger swirling sound).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, the lid fell back in place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I did that to the top! I open it up (finger swirling sound).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you open it up. Make a constitution saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As it (cloud explosion) sprays in the air--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It does it again?! It didn't expend?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: These don't!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I would've thought there'd be a little container--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They reset when they close!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With what?! Are there a bunch of gnomes down there?!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's like a paintball gun.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: On a lockpick DC of 30, you manage to somehow find the intricacy of a king dwarf sarcophagus sealed door. It takes you a good two to three minutes to carefully find the proper tumblers and hold them without it breaking or ruining the lock, but you manage to feel something shift, and a slight latch mechanism on the other side go (creaking). At which point, the brass door from the front door's jaw goes (door opening) and pours forth this green fume that fills the entirety of the chamber. Scanlan, Pike, and Vax, I need you and you because you're still in the back of the chamber--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look at that face.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you mind if I give a second opinion?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, what could it hurt? Oh, not that good though. No, lower than what you rolled.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. There's no traps.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's okay, you're good now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We need a key though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does appear to be locked, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, maybe you should pick the lock.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We need a key. Let me try my--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, let's just pick his for this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pick it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pick the lock? Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm not in this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm in the back, ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike is, though. She's been doing this for five minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you and Pike, you're in the back of the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm right by the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's everyone doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Outside the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm outside, waiting for a call.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm right next to him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So it's a total of what?
